page: Microsoft Azure E8as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the EASv5 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable, and has no onboard GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth present notable tradeoffs. While cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth is average, large-block read-only and write-only memory bandwidths fall into the bottom 25%, indicating potential bottlenecks during large, uncached memory transfers. Similarly, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM prompt processing speeds are relatively weak, whereas single-threaded tasks, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8as_v5 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without the need for high-density local storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, caching layers like Redis, medium-scale web serving, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, a 3.525 GHz clock speed, and 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance, though large-block memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression are weaker. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high-speed local disk access. The E8as_v5 is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8as_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. It delivers strong single-core performance for databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8as_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, ideal for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a high memory-to-core ratio for database and caching workloads.
